official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, decode, and observe the container id, data id, and value body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound container_set_data frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns container_set_data immediately after container_set_content.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the container_set_data id to ClientboundContainerSetDataPacket.
The official container_set_data body is containerId, id, and value in STREAM_CODEC order.
The fixture uses the official ClientboundContainerSetDataPacket constructor with numeric fields only, so it does not require an initialized Menu, screen, Level, inventory, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded container id, decoded data id, decoded value, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove menu property semantics, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer menu property semantics, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
